title: EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE - 211info
text: CENTER FOR HUMAN DEVELOPMENT EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rapid identification, support, assessment and treatment for teenagers and young adults who are experiencing the early signs of psychosis. Services include education about psychosis; medication along with medication monitoring; individual and group counseling; multi-family groups; occupational therapy; family education and support; vocational training and life-skills coaching; 24/7 crisis services; connections to community resources. 2301 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Ages 12-30 who have had a first episode of psychosis within the last 12 months or who are experiencing early at-risk symptoms for psychosis and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 962-8800 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 962-8800 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for intake FEES: Accepts OHP and private insurances. Sliding scale fees available for uninsured patients. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable AREA SERVED: Union County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8-5:30pm Services: Early Intervention for Mental Illness Other Services Offered At This Location: HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH PROGRAM CLINIC INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CENTER FOR HUMAN DEVELOPMENT
